operations.value: Evidence for glycine as a sleep aid remains preliminary: trials are few, generally small, short, and concentrated in adults with subjective sleep dissatisfaction rather than patients with formally diagnosed chronic insomnia.
operations.notes: Central evidence limitation based on the small sleep-trial base and broader systematic review.

sources.title: The effect of glycine administration on the characteristics of physiological systems in human adults: A systematic review
Subjective effects of glycine ingestion before bedtime on sleep quality
Glycine ingestion improves subjective sleep quality in human volunteers, correlating with polysomnographic changes

notes: Prevents the page from treating glycine as an established insomnia therapy.
